purpose: Defines the permitted triage dispositions for an AI-assisted device-protection claims workflow. The system provides recommendations and routing; it does not make final payment, repair authorization, fraud, or customer-closure decisions.

primary_dispositions.definition: Available facts indicate the claim is within policy coverage, required evidence is present, and no manual-review trigger exists.
primary_dispositions.system_action: Route to the standard repair/replacement/claims-processing path.
primary_dispositions.example_trigger: Active policy, matching device, covered accidental damage, claim limit available, and valid required evidence.

primary_dispositions.definition: The claim may be eligible, but one or more material eligibility facts or required supporting evidence are missing or unclear.
primary_dispositions.system_action: Hold the claim and generate only targeted follow-up questions.
primary_dispositions.example_trigger: Policy appears active and incident type may be covered, but incident date, IMEI, photos, or required repair quote is missing.

primary_dispositions.definition: A conflict, exception, anomaly, unsupported scenario, or rule gap means the system should not make a deterministic recommendation.
primary_dispositions.system_action: Route to a claims analyst with facts, applicable rules, and the review reason.
primary_dispositions.example_trigger: Claim narrative conflicts with structured data, device mismatch, date anomaly, possible duplicate claim, or policy exception.

primary_dispositions.definition: A verified policy rule conclusively shows the claim is not covered. Final decline communication remains subject to human/process confirmation.
primary_dispositions.system_action: Provide rule-grounded reason and route for decline confirmation.
primary_dispositions.example_trigger: Policy inactive on incident date, claim category explicitly excluded, device is not covered, claim limit exhausted, or waiting period clearly applies.

manual_review_reasons.description: Structured facts, evidence, or claim narrative materially conflict.
Claimed device identifier differs from the enrolled covered device.
Incident, quote, repair, or policy dates are inconsistent or require interpretation.
Claim history indicates a pattern requiring analyst assessment.
Repair/replacement value exceeds a defined operational threshold.
Goodwill, exception, or special operational handling is requested or required.
Product, geography, policy version, or incident scenario is outside the supported rule set.
A materially similar recent claim or evidence item is detected.

supporting_evidence: Identity or ownership confirmation where required
information_classes.rule: A clearly failed coverage rule should produce NOT_ELIGIBLE without requesting irrelevant supporting evidence. Missing information should normally produce INFO_REQUIRED rather than an automatic decline.

decision_precedence.condition: System conflict, exception, material inconsistency, anomaly, unsupported scenario, or unavailable rule
A minimum eligibility fact is missing or unclear
A verified hard policy rule clearly fails
Eligibility appears met but required supporting evidence is missing or unclear
All mandatory checks pass and no review trigger exists

safety_boundaries: The workflow is AI-assisted triage, not autonomous final claims adjudication.
LLM confidence alone must not determine a disposition.
Structured policy rules are authoritative for coverage determinations.
Conflicting sources must be routed to MANUAL_REVIEW; the system must not guess.
Risk signals can trigger MANUAL_REVIEW but must never independently trigger NOT_ELIGIBLE.
Every result must include disposition, facts used, rules applied, missing information, and manual-review reason where applicable.
